items.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels threaten to resume attacks on US vessels if it joins Israel’s campaign on Iran
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Houthi rebels in Yemen threatened to resume attacks on U.S. vessels and warships if the Trump administration joined Israel’s campaign on Iran. Saturday’s threat came in a pre-recorded video statement by the Houthi military spokesman Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Mahmoud Khalil vows to continue protesting Israel's war in Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Former Columbia University student Mahmoud Khalil arrived in Newark, New Jersey on Saturday, a day after leaving a federal immigration facility in Louisiana where he spent 104 days in custody for protesting against Israel's war in Gaza.

items.title: Belarus frees dissident Siarhei Tsikhanouski and 13 others after a rare visit from top US envoy
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Belarus has freed Siarhei Tsikhanouski, a key dissident figure and the husband of exiled opposition leader Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya, and 13 others following a rare visit by a senior U.S. official, Tsikhanouskaya’s team announced on Saturday.

items.content:
<article>A spokesperson for the Fire Department in Northern Israel said on Saturday that an Iranian drone hit a building in northern Israel causing damage but no injuries.

items.title: Long Island's last duck farm rebuilds after a flu outbreak forced the culling of its entire flock
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Long Island’s last Duck Farm was a casualty of the global avian flu outbreak, one of many farms around the U.S. that had to cull their entire flock, sending the prices of eggs and other agricultural commodities soaring.

Now the century-old farm is cautiously rebuilding.

#shorts #duckfarm #longisland #birdflu</article>

items.title: Turkey’s president says Iran's response to Israel strikes is 'legitimate'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Iran's response to Israeli strikes on its territory was "legitimate and legal," while condemning Israel's actions as "banditry," says Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

items.title: How to stay cool during a heat wave
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The first big heat wave of 2025 is pushing temperatures into the 90s and beyond across much of the U.S. From staying cool at night to recognizing signs of heat illness, here’s what experts say you need to know.

items.title: Luxury superyacht is partially resurfaced 10 months after sinking off Sicily
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A British-flagged luxury superyacht that sank off Sicily last year, killing U.K. tech magnate Mike Lynch and six others, resurfaced Saturday as salvage recovery crews finalized the complex operation to bring it ashore for further investigation. (AP video shot by Giuseppe Giordano)
